hook.text: A company spends $50 million on a new factory. That's not an expense—it's an investment.

buildup.text: Investing activities track cash spent on or received from long-term assets.

discovery.text: Buying equipment, acquiring businesses, and selling investments all appear in this section.

twist.text: Negative investing cash flow is often good—it means the company is investing in its future.

climax.text: But if a company is always selling assets to fund operations, that's a distress signal.

punchline.text: Spending on the future looks bad today but pays off tomorrow.

quiz.question: When is negative investing cash flow a good sign?
options: When the company is investing in growth
